hero.title: Sustainable development through local value creation in Africa
hero.subtitle: We promote climate-resilient livelihoods, food security and nature conservation through integrated, community-led projects

projectsPage.title: Our Projects
projectsPage.programLabel: Moringa Circular Economy Programme · Kilifi County, Kenya
projectsPage.lead: One programme, five independent projects. Each stands on its own and can be funded individually or together.
projectsPage.intro: In Kilifi County on the Kenyan coast, Mimea Impact is building a holistic livelihoods programme centred on the Moringa plant. Rather than one single large project, we have structured the programme into five clearly defined projects. This lets foundations take on a whole project while private donors can contribute any amount.
projectsPage.chooseText: Each project shows transparently what it costs, whom it reaches and what it achieves.
projectsPage.provenText: All five projects have already been developed, tested and successfully implemented in Kilifi County under the World Bank programme KEMFSED. Our local implementation partner is the women-led Vuka Mama Initiative. Mimea Impact's project lead is regularly on site and has personally verified their effectiveness.
projectsPage.farmText: All projects build on a 10-acre (4-hectare) community farm in Kwademu, Kaloleni. The land belongs to the local partner and stays permanently in her hands. A shared processing centre links the projects into a circular economy: every by-product becomes the raw material for the next step.
projectsPage.budgetTitle: How the overall programme breaks down
projectsPage.budgetNote: Total budget CHF 150'000 over 24 months. A detailed line-item budget is available on request.
projectsPage.mgmtNote: On-site programme coordination, monitoring, financial reporting and quality assurance.

kinderernaehrung.title: Child Nutrition
kinderernaehrung.tagline: Moringa leaf powder against malnutrition in children.
kinderernaehrung.heroSubtitle: Nutrient-rich Moringa flour for children and mothers in Kilifi.
kinderernaehrung.challenge: More than 30% of children under five in Kilifi County suffer from chronic malnutrition, with acute deficiencies in iron, zinc and vitamin A. Affordable, nutrient-rich food is scarce.
kinderernaehrung.what: Moringa leaf powder is rich in iron, zinc, vitamin A, calcium and protein. It is added to maize flour (Ugali), the daily staple food. The enriched flour is distributed in affordable sachets through schools, health stations, community health workers and local vendors. Three Community Interest Groups (around 75 households) are directly involved as Moringa suppliers.
results: A reliable supply of affordable, nutrient-rich food supplements for children and mothers
Goal: reducing the prevalence of anaemia by 20% within 18 months
Income opportunities for smallholder Moringa producers
A scalable model that can be transferred to other regions facing food insecurity

frauenfoerderung.title: Women's Empowerment
frauenfoerderung.tagline: A women-led workshop for natural cosmetics made from Moringa.
frauenfoerderung.heroSubtitle: Independent income and entrepreneurial skills for 40 women.
frauenfoerderung.challenge: 47.5% of women in rural Kilifi live below the poverty line. They carry the main responsibility for the family yet have only limited access to their own income and vocational training.
frauenfoerderung.what: A women-led production workshop makes soaps, body oils, balms and natural cosmetics based on Moringa seed oil (Ben oil), a high-quality, skin-nourishing raw material. The women are trained in oil extraction, soap making, quality control, packaging and business management. The products are sold through local markets, tourism shops along the Kilifi County coast and community networks.
results: 40 women trained in production and business management
A sustainable income source of KES 5'000 to 10'000 in additional monthly income per participant
Affordable natural body-care products for the local population
A women's cooperative with its own bookkeeping

tierfutter.title: Circular Animal Feed
tierfutter.tagline: Moringa by-products become protein-rich animal feed.
tierfutter.heroSubtitle: Nothing is wasted: residues become valuable feed.
tierfutter.challenge: Moringa processing generates substantial by-products: around 2'800 kg in the first year and over 5'000 kg from the third year, roughly 70% of the entire dry harvest. Left unused, this would be pure waste.
tierfutter.what: Instead of disposing of this biomass, it is processed into protein-rich feed pellets, blended at a ratio of 25 to 35% with locally abundant coconut and cassava waste. The result is affordable, high-quality feed for the region.
results: Waste-free processing: all Moringa by-products are turned into valuable feed
Feed costs reduced by 30 to 40% compared with imported concentrates
Improved yields in poultry, dairy cattle and fish farming
Less environmental pressure through extensive grazing

baeckerei.title: Community Bakery
baeckerei.tagline: A bakery running on biogas instead of charcoal, run by 30 women.
baeckerei.heroSubtitle: Moringa bread from renewable energy, powered by a women's group.
baeckerei.targetGroup: 1 self-help group (30 women), around 200 households through sales
baeckerei.challenge: Widespread charcoal production for cooking fuel drives deforestation and threatens fragile coastal ecosystems. At the same time, women lack viable sources of income.
baeckerei.what: A community bakery produces Moringa-enriched bread, Mandazi and Chapati. Its main fuel is biogas from the processing centre's biogas plant, so charcoal becomes unnecessary. A self-help group of 30 women runs the bakery and is trained in production, food hygiene, costing and sales. It is a proven model from the KEMFSED programme.
results: 30 women run an economically viable bakery that serves around 200 households daily
The biogas oven replaces charcoal and thus protects the coastal forests
Organic fertiliser ("bio-slurry") is produced as a by-product and distributed to outgrower farmers
An estimated CO₂ reduction of 2 to 4 tonnes per year

bioanbau.title: Organic Farming
bioanbau.tagline: Organic certification for 50 women and men farmers.
bioanbau.heroSubtitle: Higher prices and transferable knowledge through certified organic farming.
bioanbau.challenge: Smallholder farmers sell their raw products to middlemen at rock-bottom prices. Without certification and expertise, they remain at the bottom of the value chain.
bioanbau.what: The project trains smallholder farmers in organic farming methods and prepares them for group organic certification through an internal control system (ICS). The training covers soil management, composting, chemical-free pest control, bookkeeping and quality assurance. Once certified, the farmers can supply organic Moringa leaves and apply the same methods to other crops.
results: 50 farmers trained in organic farming methods
Group organic certification with a price premium of 15 to 25% for certified organic Moringa
Transferable skills that go beyond Moringa
An internal control system for traceability and quality assurance

programm.title: Programme Management & Impact Measurement
programm.heroSubtitle: So that every franc arrives where it is meant to make a difference.
programm.challenge: Impact does not happen by itself. It requires on-site coordination, careful measurement and honest reporting to funders.
programm.what: On-site programme coordination, monitoring and evaluation, financial reporting and quality assurance. The project lead is present in Kilifi County regularly and for extended periods, working side by side with the Vuka Mama team. All international payments are subject to the four-eyes principle.
results: Direct steering and quality control on site rather than from afar
Reporting based on personal observation
Dual authorisation of all international payments (four-eyes principle)
Impact measurement against clear indicators

packageDetail.sharedText: This project is part of the Moringa Circular Economy in Kilifi County. It is implemented together with the women-led Vuka Mama Initiative and proven under the World Bank programme KEMFSED.
packageDetail.ctaText: Foundations can take on the whole project, while private donors can contribute any amount.

about.title: About Mimea Impact
about.intro: Mimea Impact is a Swiss non-profit association founded in 2026, dedicated to sustainable development in East Africa. We believe in an integrated approach that combines community development, food security, nature conservation and climate resilience.
about.introText: Our work is guided by scientific principles, strong local ownership and full transparency. We work closely with local communities, academic institutions and partner organisations to create sustainable and measurable impact.
about.missionText: Our mission is to support local entrepreneurship and livelihoods through activities related to Moringa and other local plant crops. We combine these economic opportunities with essential environmental goals: preserving biodiversity and reducing conflicts between communities and wildlife.
about.boardIntro: Our board brings diverse expertise in governance, law and international project management.
about.teamIntro: Our field team combines academic expertise with deep local knowledge for effective and culturally appropriate implementation.

teamMember1.name: Fabrice Volken, MSc ETH
teamMember1.bio: Fabrice is, among other things, the founder of Paperwork AG and of the non-profit foundation Büro-Spitex. He has always combined entrepreneurial action with social commitment. He currently works in botanical raw materials and natural cosmetics, bringing directly relevant expertise to the Moringa programme.

teamMember2.name: Esther Mrabu
teamMember2.bio: Esther Mrabu has 14 years of experience in Kilifi County, including in organic Moringa farming, outgrower support and microfinance training. She leads the Vuka Mama Initiative ("Rise up, Mother" in Swahili), a women-led organisation that managed Moringa production on the ground in the World Bank project KEMFSED.

legalPage.legalFormText: Association under Art. 60 ff. of the Swiss Civil Code (ZGB). Recognised as charitable and exempt from taxation by the Canton of Zurich Tax Office.
legalPage.liabilityText: Mimea Impact strives to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the information on this website. However, we accept no liability for the timeliness, accuracy or completeness of the content. Links to third-party websites are provided for information only; we have no control over their content and accept no responsibility for it.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.generalText: We take the protection of your personal data seriously. This website is operated in accordance with the Swiss Data Protection Act (nFADP) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
privacyPage.dataCollectedText: We only collect personal data that you provide to us voluntarily:
privacyPage.dataContact: Contact form: name, email address and your message. This data is used solely to respond to your enquiry.
privacyPage.dataNewsletter: Newsletter: your email address, used exclusively to send you updates about our projects. You can unsubscribe at any time.
privacyPage.cookiesText: This website uses only technically necessary cookies (e.g. language preference). We do not use tracking cookies, analytics tools or advertising technologies.
privacyPage.thirdPartiesText: This website is hosted by Infomaniak (Geneva, Switzerland). Infomaniak may process server log data (IP address, browser type, access time) for security purposes. No other third-party services are integrated.
privacyPage.rightsText: You have the right to access, correct or delete your personal data at any time. Please contact us at info@mimea-impact.ch.
privacyPage.lawText: Swiss law applies. The place of jurisdiction is Adliswil, Canton of Zurich.

statutesPage.title: Statutes
statutesPage.intro: Below is a summary of the key articles of the Mimea Impact statutes, adopted at the founding assembly. The full statutes are available on request.
statutesPage.nameText: Under the name "Mimea Impact", an association exists within the meaning of Art. 60 ff. of the Swiss Civil Code (ZGB).
statutesPage.seatText: The association's registered office is in Adliswil, Canton of Zurich.
statutesPage.purposeText: The association pursues non-profit and charitable purposes. It promotes sustainable development in East Africa, in particular through local value creation, food security, women's empowerment and environmental protection.
statutesPage.membershipText: Any natural or legal person who supports the association's purpose may become a member. Admission is decided by the board.
statutesPage.organsText: The bodies of the association are: the General Assembly, the Board and the Auditors.
statutesPage.assemblyText: The General Assembly is the association's supreme body. It meets at least once a year and decides on amendments to the statutes, the budget, the annual accounts and the election of board members.
statutesPage.boardText: The board consists of at least three members elected by the General Assembly. It manages the association's affairs, represents it externally and may delegate operational tasks to a project team.
statutesPage.financesText: The association's resources come from membership fees, donations, grants and income from its activities. Board members serve on a voluntary basis.
statutesPage.dissolutionText: In the event of dissolution, the association's assets are transferred to a tax-exempt organisation with a similar purpose. Distribution among members is excluded.
statutesPage.fullVersion: The full statutes are available on request at info@mimea-impact.ch.

footer.description: We strengthen rural communities in East Africa through sustainable development and local value creation.
footer.newsletterText: Receive updates on our project progress
